payload.audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

content_quality.default_object_model: Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.
content_quality.guide_assumption: The reader is using dali-ui to build applications.

code_verification: receiver method exists on the receiver type
static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
assignment target is compatible with the method return type
argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
enum owner and enum value both exist
property constants use the correct owner and value type
signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone
draft_requirements: Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
repair_requirements: Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Create and configure the primary object using public dali-ui APIs.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Prefer typed constructors and setters when available.

usage_patterns.intent: React to user or resource events through public signals.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use signal connection APIs only with their owning trait or class.

rendered_prompt:
You are reviewing an existing DALi dali-ui application developer guide draft.
This is Phase 2.3.5: source-grounded prose review.

Primary goal:
- Improve natural-language accuracy and guide flow using repository evidence.
- Preserve the document's useful structure and examples whenever possible.
- Do not perform a full rewrite.
- Keep the revised user-facing guide in en only.
- Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose from the revised guide.

Feature target:
- Feature: Animated Image View (animated-image-view)
- Category: uncategorized
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Review rules:
- Review every natural-language paragraph line by line against public headers, implementation files, samples, and UTCs when needed.
- Accuracy is the first priority. Flow is the second priority.
- Edit the smallest unit needed: word, sentence, paragraph, or section-local addition/removal.
- If a word-level change is enough, change only the word.
- If a sentence-level change is enough, change only the sentence.
- If an entire paragraph is based on a wrong premise, revise, add, or remove that paragraph.
- Preserve code blocks when possible.
- If prose can be corrected to match an already accurate code block, prefer changing prose.
- If the code block itself makes the prose inaccurate or breaks the guide flow, rewrite only the smallest necessary part of that code block and verify it against public headers/source.
- Do not invent APIs.
- Do not add generic filler just to make the document longer.
- Do not change section structure unless prose_review.allow_section_restructure is true or the current prose is clearly misleading.
- Prefer localized edits when prose_review.localized_edits_only is true.
- Preserve code blocks especially when prose_review.preserve_code_blocks is true.
- Prefer Dali::Ui::View, typed setters, fluent chaining, and view-tree idioms for app-facing guidance.
- Treat dali-core and dali-adaptor APIs as reference/context unless the feature explicitly requires them.

Context summary:
{
  "content_quality": {
    "overview_style": "very_short",
    "require_table_of_contents": true,
    "require_rich_api_examples": true,
    "require_backticks_for_api_names": true,
    "topic_sections_must_be_feature_specific": true,
    "avoid_mechanical_api_listing": true,
    "default_object_model": "Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.",
    "guide_assumption": "The reader is using dali-ui to build applications."
  },
  "prose_review": {
    "enabled": true,
    "apply_to": "all",
    "preserve_code_blocks": true,
    "localized_edits_only": true,
    "allow_section_restructure": false,
    "output_review_report": true,
    "fail_open_on_parse_error": true
  },

      "dali-ui": 136

---
title: Animated Image View
sidebar_label: Animated Image View
category: uncategorized
---

# Animated Image View

`AnimatedImageView` displays animated image resources such as GIF and WebP files, with full playback control including play, pause, stop, and frame-by-frame navigation.

## Table of Contents

- [Creating an AnimatedImageView](#creating-an-animatedimageview)
- [Playback Control](#playback-control)
- [Loop and Speed Configuration](#loop-and-speed-configuration)
- [Frame Array Animation](#frame-array-animation)
- [Visual Appearance](#visual-appearance)
- [Alpha Masking](#alpha-masking)
- [Loading Configuration](#loading-configuration)
- [Signals](#signals)

## Creating an AnimatedImageView

Create an `AnimatedImageView` using `AnimatedImageView::New()` with an optional URL to an animated image file:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui;

// Create with a GIF file
AnimatedImageView animatedView = AnimatedImageView::New("animations/spinner.gif");

// Create empty and set URL later
AnimatedImageView animatedView = AnimatedImageView::New();
animatedView.SetResourceUrl("animations/loading.webp");
```

Set the view size and add it to your layout:

```cpp
animatedView.SetRequestedWidth(200.0f)
             .SetRequestedHeight(200.0f);
```

## Playback Control

Control animation playback using `Play()`, `Pause()`, and `Stop()`:

```cpp
// Start or resume playback
animatedView.Play();

// Pause at current frame
animatedView.Pause();

// Stop and reset based on StopBehavior
animatedView.Stop();
```

Jump directly to a specific frame using `JumpToFrame()`:

```cpp
// Jump to frame 5
animatedView.JumpToFrame(5);
```

Query the current playback state with `GetPlayState()`:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui::AnimatedImage;

PlayState state = animatedView.GetPlayState();
if (state == PlayState::PLAYING)
{
  // Animation is actively playing
}
else if (state == PlayState::PAUSED)
{
  // Animation is paused
}
else if (state == PlayState::STOPPED)
{
  // Animation is stopped
}
```

Track the current frame and total frame count:

```cpp
int currentFrame = animatedView.GetCurrentFrame();
int totalFrames = animatedView.GetTotalFrame();
```

## Loop and Speed Configuration

### Loop Count

Set how many times the animation repeats using `SetLoopCount()`:

```cpp
// Loop infinitely (default)
animatedView.SetLoopCount(-1);

// Play exactly 3 times
animatedView.SetLoopCount(3);

// Play once
animatedView.SetLoopCount(1);
```

### Frame Speed Factor

Control playback speed with `SetFrameSpeedFactor()`. Values below 1.0 slow down the animation; values above 1.0 speed it up:

```cpp
// Half speed
animatedView.SetFrameSpeedFactor(0.5f);

// Normal speed (default)
animatedView.SetFrameSpeedFactor(1.0f);

// Double speed
animatedView.SetFrameSpeedFactor(2.0f);
```

### Frame Delay Override

Override the embedded frame timing with `SetFrameDelay()`:

```cpp
// Set 500ms between frames
animatedView.SetFrameDelay(500);
```

### Stop Behavior

Configure which frame displays when the animation stops using `SetStopBehavior()`:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui::AnimatedImage;

// Show current frame when stopped
animatedView.SetStopBehavior(StopBehavior::CURRENT_FRAME);

// Show first frame when stopped
animatedView.SetStopBehavior(StopBehavior::FIRST_FRAME);

// Show last frame when stopped
animatedView.SetStopBehavior(StopBehavior::LAST_FRAME);
```

## Frame Array Animation

Instead of a single animated file, provide an array of individual image URLs for frame-by-frame animation:

```cpp
Dali::Vector<Dali::String> frameUrls;
frameUrls.PushBack("frames/frame-001.png");
frameUrls.PushBack("frames/frame-002.png");
frameUrls.PushBack("frames/frame-003.png");
frameUrls.PushBack("frames/frame-004.png");

animatedView.SetResourceUrls(frameUrls);
```

Configure batch loading and caching for frame arrays:

```cpp
// Pre-load 4 frames at a time
animatedView.SetBatchSize(4);

// Cache up to 10 frames
animatedView.SetCacheSize(10);
```

## Visual Appearance

### Fitting Mode

Control how the image fits within the view bounds using `SetFittingMode()`:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui::Image;

// Scale to fit, preserve aspect ratio
animatedView.SetFittingMode(FittingMode::FIT_KEEP_ASPECT_RATIO);

// Stretch to fill the view
animatedView.SetFittingMode(FittingMode::FILL);

// Scale to cover, crop overflow
animatedView.SetFittingMode(FittingMode::OVER_FIT_KEEP_ASPECT_RATIO);

// Center at original size
animatedView.SetFittingMode(FittingMode::CENTER);
```

### Image Color

Apply a color tint to the animation:

```cpp
// Apply blue tint
animatedView.SetImageColor(UiColor(0x4A90E2));
```

### Sampling Mode

Set the filtering mode used when scaling:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui::Image;

// Smooth scaling
animatedView.SetSamplingMode(SamplingMode::LINEAR);

// Pixelated appearance
animatedView.SetSamplingMode(SamplingMode::NEAREST);

// High quality
animatedView.SetSamplingMode(SamplingMode::LANCZOS);
```

### Desired Size Hints

Provide size hints to the image loader:

```cpp
animatedView.SetDesiredWidth(256);
animatedView.SetDesiredHeight(256);
```

### Pixel Area

Display a sub-region of the image using normalized coordinates:

```cpp
// Show the top-left quadrant
animatedView.SetPixelArea(Vector4(0.0f, 0.0f, 0.5f, 0.5f));
```

## Alpha Masking

Apply an alpha mask to shape the visible region of the animation:

```cpp
// Set the mask image
animatedView.SetAlphaMaskUrl("masks/circle.png");

// Crop output to mask bounds
animatedView.SetCropToMask(true);
```

Choose when masking is applied using `SetMaskingMode()`:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui::Image;

// Apply mask during rendering (can be changed dynamically)
animatedView.SetMaskingMode(MaskingType::MASKING_ON_RENDERING);

// Apply mask during loading (more efficient, cannot be changed)
animatedView.SetMaskingMode(MaskingType::MASKING_ON_LOADING);
```

## Loading Configuration

### Load Policy

Control when the image loads using `SetLoadPolicy()`:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui::Image;

// Load immediately when created
animatedView.SetLoadPolicy(LoadPolicy::IMMEDIATE);

// Load when attached to the scene (default)
animatedView.SetLoadPolicy(LoadPolicy::ATTACHED);
```

### Release Policy

Control when the texture is released from cache using `SetReleasePolicy()`:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui::Image;

// Release when detached from scene
animatedView.SetReleasePolicy(ReleasePolicy::DETACHED);

// Release when view is destroyed
animatedView.SetReleasePolicy(ReleasePolicy::DESTROYED);

// Never release from cache
animatedView.SetReleasePolicy(ReleasePolicy::NEVER);
```

### Synchronous Loading

Load the image on the main thread, blocking until complete:

```cpp
animatedView.SetSynchronousLoading(true);
```

### Placeholder Image

Display a placeholder while the main image loads:

```cpp
animatedView.SetPlaceholderUrl("placeholders/loading.png");
```

## Signals

### Animation Finished Signal

Receive a callback when the animation completes all loops:

```cpp
animatedView.AnimationFinishedSignal().Connect(this, &MyClass::OnAnimationFinished);

// Handler signature
void OnAnimationFinished(Dali::Ui::View view)
{
  // Animation has finished all loops
}
```

### Resource Ready Signal

Receive a callback when the image finishes loading:

```cpp
animatedView.ResourceReadySignal().Connect(this, &MyClass::OnResourceReady);

// Handler signature
void OnResourceReady(Dali::Ui::View view)
{
  // Image is ready to display
  int totalFrames = animatedView.GetTotalFrame();
}
```

Check the loading status at any time:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui::Visual;

ResourceStatus status = animatedView.GetLoadingStatus();
